meta.title: rect

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: rect
root.mentor_note: rect keeps a moral sense of straight as well as a physical one, and both senses show up in its words.

attempt.direction_prompt: To leave an error uncorrected, or to correct it?

context_sentences: The bank promised to rectify the error within two business days.
It took a formal complaint, not a phone call, before anyone bothered to rectify the mistake.
The old judge was known for a rectitude that made even powerful defendants uneasy.
His reputation for rectitude was the one thing the scandal never quite managed to touch.
The city’s old quarter was a maze, but the new district was laid out in a strict rectilinear grid.
The physics problem assumed rectilinear motion, ignoring any curve in the object’s path.

construct_options.text: Moving in, or bounded by, straight lines
Curved, following the shape of a circle

context_sentences: Only one of the five candidates gave the correct answer without hesitating.
The final figure in the report turned out to be correct after all, despite everyone’s doubts.

construct_options.text: Free from error; in agreement with the facts
Moving in a perfectly straight physical line
Left uncorrected, despite a known error
